data.body:
[G3 container review — parent 4fcd, child 5a6c] Child coverage: CLEAN, no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent 4fcd's criteria against this child (with sibling cross-check across the full roster: 22bf, 5a6c, a36b):

- Parent SC1 / AC1 ("a mechanism DETECTS material drift in the code the review was grounded against — resolved edit-set / cited files"): w.r.t. THIS child = STRUCTURAL (out of scope by design). 5a6c is Story 2 and depends_on Story 1 (22bf); it consumes drift detection rather than providing it ("When Story 1 detects code drift…"). NOT an uncovered-parent finding: sibling 22bf FULLY covers it (AC: "binds a SIGNED {path: content-hash} map of the dependency set (file_impact ∪ review-cited files); whole-HEAD exact-match staleness check is removed").

- Parent AC2 ("drift invalidates/flags the attestation, prompting re-review; cheap enough not to tax hot paths"): w.r.t. THIS child = PARTIALLY/orthogonal. 5a6c refines the re-review remedy (narrowed re-run of {P2,P3} ∪ applicable CODEBASE_GROUNDED, Pass-3 fail-closed re-aggregation, chained attestation) and carries its own cheapness commitment (measurement AC, the 4 expensive code agents still re-run). The invalidate/flag→prompt-re-review core is FULLY covered by sibling 22bf (drift → block the claim → re-review). No gap.

- Parent AC3 ("tested: code drift after a signed review → attestation no longer valid"): w.r.t. THIS child = PARTIALLY (5a6c adds tests for the narrowed-path verdict/reuse/skew behavior). The exact parent scenario (drift after sign → invalid) is FULLY covered by sibling 22bf's test AC. No gap.

Coverage map (parent criterion → covering sibling):
- SC1/AC1 → 22bf (full); 5a6c structural (consumer).
- AC2 → 22bf (full, invalidate/flag/re-review + cheapness); 5a6c (orthogonal remedy refinement).
- AC3 → 22bf (full); 5a6c (additional narrowed-path tests).

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children, and this child introduces no coverage hole. Empty findings for G3 on this pairing.
